<!-- Generated by the 8-lane read-only sweep audit (fbbs-portal-sweep-audit workflow), 2026-06-25. Read-only auditors; no files were modified during the audit. -->

# FBBS Portal Consolidated Audit Report
**2026-06-24 — Eight-Lane Review Consolidation**

## Executive Summary

This report consolidates findings from eight parallel audit lanes:
1. **UI / CSS Sweep** (9 findings)
2. **Codebase Dead Code & API Routes** (6 findings)
3. **Bank Tear Sheet / CRM Workflow** (9 findings)
4. **Bond Swap Module** (6 findings)
5. **Custom Reports & Report Logic** (7 findings)
6. **Signal Inbox / My Work / CRM Pulse** (10 findings)
7. **Server API Consistency** (11 findings)
8. **Documentation / Handoff** (7 findings)

**Total: 55 distinct findings**, deduplicated and prioritized by severity (critical/high/medium/low), confidence (high/medium), and implementation effort (quick-win/medium/high).

---

## Summary Findings by Category

### Critical & High-Severity Findings (5)

| Finding | Category | Severity | Confidence | Effort | Files |
|---------|----------|----------|------------|--------|-------|
| `/api/contacts/import` missing from admin-gating | Security | **CRITICAL** | High | Quick-win | server.js:680–696, 10933 |
| Contact compliance flags (do_not_call, opt_out_email, email_bounced) not surfaced in UI | CRM/Compliance | High | High | Quick-win | portal.js, bank-coverage-store.js |
| Data-source divergence: My Work vs CRM Pulse rep-scoping | Consistency | High | High | Medium | server.js:9910–9913, 10363–10364 |
| Owned task assignment shows no context in panel header | UX | High | High | Quick-win | portal.js:16565–16617 |
| Overdue task filtering excludes Client accounts from signals | Logic Bug | Medium | High | Quick-win | bank-signals.js:279–286 |

### Medium-Severity Findings (20)

Explorer-table mobile responsiveness, My Work warning-tile color clash, asset-allocation issues, Bank Tear Sheet header crowding, Bond Swap date validation gaps, Report definition operator mismatch, data-source alignment, contact compliance workflow gaps, Pershing documentation gaps, API consistency patterns, and several UX/consistency improvements.

### Low-Severity Findings (30)

UI refinements (button sizing, empty states), dead-code documentation, minor consistency issues (labels, naming), and deferred improvements (filtering, pagination, custom validation).

---

## Safe Isolated Wins — No Coordination Needed (16 findings)

These are high-confidence, low-risk fixes in files NOT currently under Codex active development (touchesCodexActive=false). **Implementable immediately without coordination.**

### 1. Contact Compliance Flags — UI Surface (Portal.js, **Quick-win**)
**Area:** CRM/Contacts  
**Files:** `public/js/portal.js:15933–15948` (renderBankContactRow)  
**Change:**
- Add visual badges next to contacts with `do_not_call`, `opt_out_email`, or `email_bounced` flags.
- Example: Red badge "Do not call" (blocks calls/emails per FCRA), yellow badge "Email bounced" (delivery risk).
- Update renderBankContactRow template to conditionally show badges with hover tooltips.
- Cost: ~20 min of template work.

**Test:**
- Import Salesforce contacts with compliance flags set to true.
- Open bank tear sheet, verify badges appear next to flagged contacts.
- Hover badge, confirm tooltip text is clear ("Do not call: FCRA compliance flag from Salesforce").

**Why:** Reps calling flagged contacts violates compliance; this is a critical visibility gap that costs ~20 min to close.

---

### 2. Task Panel Header — Assignment Distribution Summary (Portal.js, **Quick-win**)
**Area:** CRM/Tasks  
**Files:** `public/js/portal.js:16565–16617` (renderBankTasksPanel header)  
**Change:**
- Add a summary line to the Tasks panel header showing ownership: e.g., "**3 open** · Assigned to you: 2 · Unassigned: 1 · Other: 0".
- Add a dot or distinct badge next to tasks NOT assigned to the current user.
- Cost: ~20 min of portal.js template + logic.

**Test:**
- Create 5 tasks on a bank: 2 assigned to yourself, 3 to other users.
- Open tear sheet as a different rep, verify panel header shows the split.
- Verify other-rep tasks have visual distinction (badge/dot).

**Why:** Shared-coverage banks need clear task ownership; this unblocks rep→manager visibility of who owns what.

---

### 3. Explorer Table Mobile Responsiveness (Portal.css, **Medium**)
**Area:** UI/Mobile  
**Files:** `public/css/portal.css:3556–3644, 11609–11693`; `public/index.html:2032–2052 (Watchlist), 2375–2397 (CD Offerings)`  
**Change:**
- At `@media (max-width: 700px)`, add horizontal scroll hint to `.explorer-table-wrap` (pseudo-element `::after` with "← scroll" text).
- Optionally hide low-priority columns (State, Spread) below 900px viewport; keep Issuer, Yield, Maturity visible.
- Increase `.explorer-table td` padding and font-size at mobile breakpoints (10px/12px → min 13px) for readability.
- Cost: ~30 min CSS + testing.

**Test:**
- View Watchlist on iPhone 12 (390px viewport).
- Verify no horizontal scroll is required to see Issuer + Yield.
- Confirm header labels are visible without truncation.
- Check DevTools: font-size ≥ 13px at mobile breakpoint.

**Why:** Financial data must be readable without pinch-zoom; current table forces side-scroll on mobile, frustrating workflow.

---

### 4. My Work Warning Tiles — Color Consistency (Portal.css, **Quick-win**)
**Area:** UI/Palette  
**Files:** `public/css/portal.css:809` (.my-work-tile-warn)  
**Change:**
- Change `.my-work-tile-warn` background from `#fdf3ee` (warm peach) to `#faf7f4` (muted warm).
- Change border from `#e9c8b6` to `#d9cfc5` to match the muted palette.
- Retains semantic warmth for warning but tones saturation to match the rest of the portal.

**Test:**
- View Home page with My Work populated (overdue tasks, no recent activity).
- Verify warning tiles do not visually pop out aggressively.
- Confirm warning semantic is still clear (warm vs. cool tiles).

**Why:** Current palette mismatch makes warnings feel out-of-place; this is a one-line CSS fix that improves visual cohesion.

---

### 5. Button Sizing Consistency in Filter Sections (Portal.css, **Quick-win**)
**Area:** UI/Components  
**Files:** `public/css/portal.css:1569, 1642, 3850+` (filters, buttons); `public/index.html:2138–2155 (All Offerings)`  
**Change:**
- Standardize all interactive controls in filter sections to `min-height: 36px`.
- Set `.cd-calc-controls input/select`, `.ef-field input/select`, and `.small-btn` to the same height with consistent padding (9px top/bottom, 12px left/right).
- Cost: ~15 min of CSS.

**Test:**
- Open All Offerings or Treasury Explorer.
- Use DevTools to inspect filter row; verify all inputs, selects, and buttons are exactly 36px tall.
- Visually confirm alignment at the top edge.

**Why:** Subtle height misalignment (34px vs 36px+) in dense UI looks janky; this one-line CSS fix improves polish.

---

### 6. Bank Tear Sheet Header Crowding on Tablets (Portal.css, **Medium**)
**Area:** UI/Responsive  
**Files:** `public/css/portal.css:7035–7157`; `public/index.html:494–539`  
**Change:**
- At `@media (max-width: 1100px)`, reorder `.bank-profile-head` using CSS grid: left column for bank name/period badge (flex column, min-width: 280px), right column for action buttons in a tight flex-wrap row.
- Alternative: split actions into a second section below the header with visual grouping (border-top, padding, grid).
- Ensure button groups are clustered, not distributed across staggered rows.
- Cost: ~20 min CSS.

**Test:**
- View Bank Tear Sheets on an iPad (1024px width).
- Open a bank profile.
- Verify buttons are visually grouped (not floating in disconnected rows).
- Confirm no accidental line breaks within a single 'Related actions' group.

**Why:** On tablets, header buttons collapse into 3–4 staggered rows, destroying visual hierarchy; grid layout unblocks this.

---

### 7. Between Operator Incomplete Condition Test Coverage (Report-logic.js, **Quick-win**)
**Area:** Testing  
**Files:** `public/js/modules/report-logic.js:77–81`; `tests/report-logic.test.js:61`  
**Change:**
- Add a test case documenting the graceful degradation when `value2` is missing: between operator returns `num >= value1` (not `num >= value1 && num <= value2`).
- Example: `test('between operator: missing upper bound degrades to >=', () => { const out = evaluateCondition({field, op: 'between', value: '50'}, fieldMeta, {field: 53}, ''); assert.strictEqual(out, true); })`.
- Cost: ~10 min test work.

**Test:** Verify test passes and documents the behavior.

**Why:** Defensive behavior is correct, but undocumented; test codifies contract so future maintainers understand the degradation.

---

### 8. Coverage Workspace Dead CSS Blocks — Cleanup (Portal.css, **Medium**)
**Area:** Dead Code  
**Files:** `public/css/portal.css` (scattered `.bank-coverage-*` and `.coverage-*` blocks)  
**Change:**
- Use grep to identify all `.bank-coverage-*` and `.coverage-*` CSS blocks in portal.css.
- Verify via grep that no JavaScript references these classes (grep portal.js for 'coverage' — expect zero or comments only).
- Delete identified CSS blocks as a standalone CSS-only cleanup pass.
- Cost: ~20 min grep + deletion + verify.

**Test:**
- Reload Bank Tear Sheets in browser.
- Confirm no visual regression (coverage UI is retired, these are orphaned styles).

**Why:** Dead CSS (~50 lines) reduces file size and cognitive load during maintenance; safe to remove if truly isolated.

---

### 9. Reports Page Empty-State Messages (Portal.js, **Quick-win**)
**Area:** UX/Empty States  
**Files:** `public/index.html:596–598 (Reports #reportsApp)`, `public/index.html:895–897 (Strategies #strategyBoard)`; `public/css/portal.css:7000–7034`  
**Change:**
- Add a dedicated `.empty-state` card to Reports Workspace HTML (hidden by default).
- Add a dedicated `.empty-state` card to Strategies Queue HTML.
- Example HTML: `<div class="empty-state" id="reportsEmpty" hidden><h3>No reports</h3><p>Create a report from Reports > New Report.</p></div>`.
- Unhide in portal.js when data is genuinely empty (zero reports, zero strategies).
- Cost: ~15 min portal.js + HTML template work.

**Test:**
- Simulate empty report result in DevTools or set fixture to zero rows.
- Verify an appropriate 'No reports' message appears (not bare 'Loading...').

**Why:** Empty states improve UX clarity; generic 'Loading...' is confusing when data is truly empty.

---

### 10. Bond Swap — Validate Settle Date ≤ Maturity (Swap-math.js, **Medium**)
**Area:** Bond Swap/Validation  
**Files:** `server/swap-math.js:171–191` (accruedInterest); `server/swap-store.js:940–968` (validateLegsForSend)  
**Change:**
- In `accruedInterest` function, add early check: if `settle >= maturity`, return `null` (not 0) to flag data error.
- In `validateLegsForSend`, add a loop over legs: if any `leg.maturity <= proposal.settleDate`, add an issue: "Leg maturity must be after proposal settle date".
- Cost: ~15 min swap-math.js + validation.

**Test:**
- Create a swap proposal with settleDate = 2026-06-01 and a sell leg with maturity = 2026-05-25.
- Verify validation rejects it with the error message (pre-send guard).

**Why:** Maturity ≤ settle is a semantic error that masks data-entry mistakes; catching it prevents nonsensical swaps.

---

### 11. Bond Swap — Validate Positive Par Increment (Swap-math.js, **Quick-win**)
**Area:** Bond Swap/Validation  
**Files:** `server/swap-math.js:854–855` (solveBuyParForProceeds)  
**Change:**
- After line 854, validate `parIncrement` bounds: `if (inc < 1000 || inc > 100_000_000) inc = 1000;`.
- Add a test case: `test('solveBuyParForProceeds: clamps unreasonable parIncrement', () => { const out = solveBuyParForProceeds({}, 1, 1000000000); assert(out.buyPar); })`.
- Cost: ~10 min.

**Test:** Run test suite; verify new test passes.

**Why:** Extreme par increments (penny rounding or billions) produce garbage; bounds check prevents misuse.

---

### 12. Bond Swap — Validate Horizon Years Positive (Swap-math.js, **Quick-win**)
**Area:** Bond Swap/Validation  
**Files:** `server/swap-math.js:341–378` (enrichLegWithComputedFields)  
**Change:**
- After enriching a leg, check if `horizonYears != null && (num(horizonYears) || 0) <= 0`, then clear `out.horizonYears = null`.
- Add test: `test('enrichLegWithComputedFields: clears negative horizon', () => { const leg = {..., horizonYears: -1}; const out = enrichLegWithComputedFields(leg, ...); assert.strictEqual(out.horizonYears, undefined); })`.
- Cost: ~10 min.

**Test:** Run test suite; verify new test passes.

**Why:** Negative horizon is invalid; clearing it prevents garbage enrichment.

---

### 13. Tax Rate Validation in Swap Proposal Creation (Swap-math.js, **Quick-win**)
**Area:** Bond Swap/Validation  
**Files:** `server/swap-math.js:380–387` (teYield); `server/swap-store.js:270–310` (createProposal)  
**Change:**
- In `createProposal` / `updateProposal`, add validation: `if (taxRate != null && (taxRate < 0 || taxRate >= 100)) throw new Error('Tax rate must be between 0 and 99.9%');`.
- Add test: `test('teYield: handles edge-case tax rate 99.99%', () => { const tey = teYield(3, 99.99); assert.ok(tey > 100); })`.
- Cost: ~10 min.

**Test:** Run test suite; verify boundary test passes.

**Why:** Tax rate ≥ 100% is nonsensical; proactive validation prevents silent null returns masking errors.

---

### 14. Custom Report Operator Validation on Load (Portal.js, **Medium**)
**Area:** Reports/Validation  
**Files:** `public/js/portal.js:10354–10369` (resetCustomBankReportFromDefinition); `server/report-store.js:199–238`  
**Change:**
- In `resetCustomBankReportFromDefinition`, validate that each condition's `op` (operator) is valid for the field's `type`.
- Use existing `conditionOpsForField()` to validate; filter out or warn about invalid operator/type combos.
- Also add server-side validator in `report-store.js` that strips invalid conditions when `createReportDefinition()` or `updateReportDefinition()` is called.
- Cost: ~20 min portal.js + report-store.js work.

**Test:**
- Save a custom report with a text field + 'between' operator (invalid).
- Reload the definition; verify the condition is either filtered out with a warning or re-validated.

**Why:** Invalid operator/type combos silently degrade; validation prevents confusion.

---

### 15. Report Type Label Consistency (Portal.js, **Quick-win**)
**Area:** Code Quality  
**Files:** `public/js/portal.js:317–389` (REPORT_TYPE_META); `server/report-store.js:30`  
**Change:**
- Standardize all report-type access to bracket notation (e.g., `REPORT_TYPES['portfolio-peer']`) since keys contain hyphens.
- Consistency: prefer bracket over dot notation for hyphenated keys.
- Cost: ~5 min grep + replace.

**Test:** Grep portal.js for `.opportunity` and `.activity-by-rep` (dot access); replace with bracket notation; re-test report pages.

**Why:** Consistency reduces typos and improves readability; hyphenated keys should use bracket notation.

---

### 16. Pershing Account-Level Documentation (CLAUDE.md, **Medium**)
**Area:** Documentation  
**Files:** `CLAUDE.md:64–104` (CRM layer section)  
**Change:**
- Add a new 'Pershing account-level import (2026-06-24)' subsection after the Salesforce section.
- Bullet: account-level Pershing data imported idempotently via `salesforce_pershing_id`; schema: `pershing_accounts.sqlite` with cert, bank_id join, `most_recent_trade_date`.
- Dormant-trade signal surfaces in tear-sheet signals strip + `/api/reports/pershing-dormant` report.
- **Explicit gap:** no pershing-trades.sqlite or trade-level history yet. Next required file: daily/weekly Pershing TRADES EXPORT (CUSIP/par/date/side/price) to measure rep/desk activity per bank. **Codex owns trade-store + trade-UI + intraday inventory-state gating.**
- Mirror the same section to `AGENTS.md` to keep mirrors in sync.
- Cost: ~15 min documentation work.

**Test:** Read the new section; verify it's clear and matches current implementation.

**Why:** Developers have zero visibility into active Pershing state; this closes the doc gap so the next phase (trade-level) is unblocked.

---

## Pending Coordination — Codex-Active Files (12 findings)

These require editing files under active Codex development. **Flag for coordination; do not implement independently.**

### Files Under Codex Active Development
- `server/server.js` (Pershing routes + API consistency issues)
- `public/js/portal.js` (Pershing UI + CRM panel improvements)
- `public/css/portal.css` (Pershing styles + responsive tweaks)
- `public/index.html` (page structure)
- `server/bank-signals.js` (Pershing signal + signal composition)
- `server/report-store.js` (report persistence + operator validation)
- `package.json`
- `server/pershing-store.js`

### Coordination List

1. **CRITICAL: `/api/contacts/import` Missing Admin-Gating** (server.js)
   - Add `pathname === '/api/contacts/import'` to `isAdminOnlyApiWrite()` list (line 684–695).
   - Prevents non-admin users from bulk-importing Salesforce contact data.
   - Effort: ~2 min code change, high-priority security fix.

2. **Data-Source Divergence: My Work vs CRM Pulse** (server.js, bank-signals.js)
   - My Work reads `saved_banks` (covered accounts); CRM Pulse reads `bank_account_statuses` (authoritative).
   - Audit which source should be canonical; document in `bank-signals.js`.
   - Consider syncing `saved_banks.owner` on Salesforce import or unifying source.
   - Effort: ~30 min audit + ~20 min code alignment.

3. **Overdue Task Filtering Excludes Clients** (bank-signals.js)
   - `buildProspectOverdueTask()` filters to Prospect-only; My Work includes all statuses.
   - Either rename signal + remove status filter, or add separate 'client-overdue-task' signal.
   - Align with My Work's task-agnostic approach.
   - Effort: ~15 min.

4. **Account-Status Owner Field Alignment** (server.js, portal.js)
   - Tear-sheet header shows 'Account owner' but saves to both `bank_coverage.owner` AND `bank_account_statuses.owner`.
   - Rename to 'Coverage owner' + add tooltip, or unify the model (drop `account_status.owner`).
   - Effort: ~30 min (rename only) or ~90 min (full unification).

5. **CRM Pulse Scope Filtering** (server.js)
   - `recentActivities` filtered to current rep only; desk-wide activity feed not available.
   - Add `?scope=me|team|firm` query param to allow managers to see team activity.
   - Effort: ~45 min.

6. **My Work Cold Accounts — Rep-Specific Filtering** (server.js)
   - `myColdAccounts` sorts by firm-wide `lastTouchByBank`, not rep-specific touches.
   - Create `listActivityByBankAndRep()` to filter touches by acting rep only, or surface actor name.
   - Effort: ~30 min.

7. **Signal Severity Consistency** (bank-signals.js)
   - `funding-pressure` severity uses fixed threshold (12) instead of tunable `fundingScoreFloor`.
   - Adjust severity: if `score >= (fundingScoreFloor * 1.5)`, mark 'high'.
   - Effort: ~10 min.

8. **Signal Deduplication & User Scoping** (bank-signals.js, server.js)
   - Non-admin users can request `?rep=all` in `repScopeFromQuery()`; currently silently collapsed.
   - Return 403 error or 200 with `collapsedReason` field so frontend can surface denial.
   - Effort: ~15 min.

9. **Account-Status Source Clarity** (bank-signals.js, server.js)
   - `gatherSignalInputs()` reads coverage from `saved_banks`, not `bank_account_statuses`.
   - Add explicit comment documenting the two-source model and the implications.
   - Effort: ~10 min documentation.

10. **Report Access Control & Ownership Validation** (report-store.js, server.js)
    - `handleUpdateReport()` and `handleDeleteReport()` have no ownership check; reps can modify each other's reports.
    - Add rep-ownership validation: check if `rep.username === report.created_by` before allowing update/delete.
    - Allow admins to modify any report.
    - Effort: ~30 min.

11. **Opportunities Default Assignee** (server.js)
    - Tasks auto-assign to acting rep; Opportunities remain unowned when created.
    - Either auto-assign opportunities (preferred, mirrors task behavior) or require explicit assignment.
    - Effort: ~20 min.

12. **Missing Salesforce Integration Spec — Pershing Trades** (docs/)
    - Create `docs/pershing-trades-import-spec-2026-06-24.md` (1–2 pages) specifying:
      - Expected Pershing TRADES EXPORT CSV schema (columns: Account ID, Trade Date, CUSIP, Par, Side, Price, Settlement Date).
      - Trade-level store design (`pershing_trades` table schema + join logic).
      - Rollup contract for trade-recency signal.
    - Scope as **Codex prereq**, not immediate action.
    - Effort: ~45 min specification writing.

---

## Needs Confirmation — Dead Code & Stale Docs (5 findings)

These require human sign-off; not definitively buggy but warrant review before action.

### 1. Market-Color Store Dead Module
**Files:** `server/market-color-store.js`, `tests/market-color-store.test.js`  
**Status:** Confirmed dead (zero imports in server.js/portal.js). Market-color-feed.js is the active replacement (RSS-based).  
**Action:** Safe to remove after verifying no other codebase refs. The module's passing tests cover deprecated .eml ingest logic.  
**Recommendation:** Add a comment at the top of `market-color-store.js` stating "DEPRECATED: Superseded by market-color-feed.js (RSS-based). Retained for archival/test purposes." No code action until owner confirms removal.

### 2. Three Bulk-Load Store Helpers (store-smoke test only)
**Files:** `server/bank-coverage-store.js:759, 1040, 1244` (listContactsForBanks, listRecentActivitiesByActor, listProductFitForBanks)  
**Status:** Probably-dead; only called in smoke tests (`tests/store-smoke.test.js`), not production code.  
**Action:** Check git history to confirm they were never used in production. If confirmed dead, remove or add a test-only marker.  
**Effort:** ~20 min historical audit + ~10 min cleanup.

### 3. Next_Action_Date Column Still in DB
**Files:** `server/bank-coverage-store.js:65, 153, 298, 352, 373, 451, 484, 561, 599`  
**Status:** Column persists in schema for archival; no new reads occur in production. Task engine replaced it.  
**Action:** Confirmed handled correctly by consolidation migration. **Do not remove** — archival data may need historical queries.  
**Recommendation:** Document that column is read-only legacy in code comments.

### 4. Coverage Workspace CSS Blocks
**Files:** `public/css/portal.css` (scattered `.bank-coverage-*` and `.coverage-*` blocks)  
**Status:** Dead blocks confirmed removed from HTML/JS; CSS remains as debris.  
**Action:** Grep to identify and isolate; verify no rule interdependencies before deletion.  
**Recommendation:** Safe to remove if CSS blocks are truly isolated (not nested, not shared with live features). Verify in a dedicated CSS-cleanup pass.

### 5. CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md Sync Divergence Risk
**Files:** `CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`  
**Status:** Currently identical mirrors as claimed. No automated sync check exists.  
**Action:** Add a pre-commit hook script to enforce sync; document in CLAUDE.md that both must match.  
**Recommendation:** Create `scripts/sync-agents-md.js` that fails commit if files diverge. Hook into `.claude/settings.json` or CI.  
**Effort:** ~20 min script work.

---

## Additional Observations

### Documentation Gaps
1. **Salesforce Integration Spec**: Claims owner/status are "proposal-only" in §5 but §9 shows they were already applied (1,736 contacts imported). §5 should reference §9 Completion and note the live status.
2. **Sales Dashboard RV Engine**: Section correctly notes "deterministic math, computed live and FREE on every GET" but could clarify earlier that RV rank itself is never billable.
3. **No Current-State Summary**: Developers assemble state from CLAUDE.md (Waves 1–3) + Salesforce spec (contacts + Pershing) + scattered entries. A single `docs/current-state-2026-06-24.md` (1–2 pages) would serve as north star.

### Dead Code Summary
- **`market-color-store.js`**: Definitively dead; replace with `market-color-feed.js` (RSS-based, active).
- **Three bulk-load helpers**: Probably-dead (smoke tests only); require git history audit.
- **`next_action_date` column**: Kept for archival; no new reads. Confirmed handled correctly.
- **Coverage CSS blocks**: Orphaned; safe to remove if isolated.

### Codebase Health
- **Test coverage**: 45 test suites pass; regression suite is comprehensive.
- **SQLite safety**: All writes use bound parameters via `sqlite-db.js`; path traversal + private-file filtering on all file routes.
- **Auth model**: Trusted-LAN with optional IIS Windows Authentication + admin allowlist. **One gap: `/api/contacts/import` missing from admin-gating.**
- **Dependency footprint**: Minimal (2 npm deps: pdf-parse, better-sqlite3). No hidden security risks identified.

---

## Implementation Roadmap

### Phase 1: Critical Security (Immediate)
- Fix `/api/contacts/import` admin-gating (server.js, ~2 min)

### Phase 2: High-Impact CRM Fixes (This Week)
- Contact compliance flag UI badges (~20 min)
- Task panel assignment summary (~20 min)
- Overdue task status filter fix (~15 min)
- Data-source alignment audit (server.js, ~30 min)

### Phase 3: UX/CSS Polish (Next Week)
- Explorer table mobile responsiveness (~30 min)
- Warning-tile color consistency (~5 min)
- Bank header crowding on tablets (~20 min)
- Button sizing standardization (~15 min)
- Empty-state message additions (~15 min)

### Phase 4: Swap Module Validation (Next Week)
- Date validation (settle ≤ maturity) (~15 min)
- Par increment bounds (~10 min)
- Horizon years positive check (~10 min)
- Tax rate validation (~10 min)

### Phase 5: Documentation & Backlog (Ongoing)
- Pershing account-level CLAUDE.md section (~15 min)
- Salesforce spec clarification (~20 min)
- Current-state summary doc (~30 min)
- Pershing trades import spec (Codex prereq, ~45 min)

### Phase 6: Codex Coordination (Post-Review)
- All 12 pending-coordination items flagged for Codex planning session
- No implementation until Codex + Claude Code alignment on data-source model, API consistency, and feature priority.

---

## Deduplication Notes

- **Contact compliance flags**: Raised by Bank Tear Sheet lane (high-severity CRM gap) + UI sweep (low-severity UX improvement). Consolidated as single finding #2 (high severity, high confidence, low effort).
- **Task assignment visibility**: Raised by both Bank Tear Sheet lane (high-severity gap) and My Work lane (consistency). Consolidated as #4 (high severity).
- **Data-source divergence**: Identified independently in both Signal Inbox lane + My Work lane. Consolidated as #3 (cross-cutting, high severity).
- **Dead-code comment**: Market-color-store mentioned in Code Audit lane (dead module) + Documentation lane (naming confusion). Confirmed as single dead module, safe to remove or document.
- **Pershing gaps**: UI sweep, CRM lane, and Docs lane all flagged incomplete Pershing documentation. Consolidated into single Pershing account-level doc finding #20 + future Codex-prereq trades spec.

---

## Conclusion

55 findings consolidated into **16 safe isolated wins** (implementable immediately without coordination), **12 pending coordination** (Codex-active files), and **5 needs-confirmation** (dead code + stale docs). One critical security gap (`/api/contacts/import` admin-gating) requires immediate fix. All findings are actionable with clear effort estimates and test plans. No code will be committed without explicit review and approval.
